text: vv. 3-10: Cf. Ps. 119.1; 17.7; 41.8; 30.23; 68.2; 141.4; 142.4; 17.6; 30.7; 116.17, 18; 3.9. The occurrence of such a cento proves how complete a body of psalms was in existence at the time when this book was written.

The temple here alluded to is presumably the temple at Jerusalem, although Jonas is usually regarded as belonging, not to Juda, but to the northern kingdom (IV Kg. 14.25). In verse 6, the Latin has ‘sea’ instead of ‘weeds’.
